Niyam — what's actually built

Every image below is the complete phone screen, captured from a real install on a Pixel 9 emulator on 11 June — exactly what a user sees, top to bottom. Scroll through in order: it follows the journey of a brand-new user.

1 · Welcome

The first screen a new user sees. App name in the serif brand font on the cream background, one orange button.

2 · Onboarding step 1 — Why are you here?

User picks their intention. The Continue button stays grey/disabled until they choose.

2b · Same screen after tapping a choice

The chosen card gets the orange outline + tint. Continue turns orange.

3 · Onboarding step 2 — Pick your starter mantra

Three starter mantras matched to the chosen intention, each with a one-line meaning and reading time.

4 · Onboarding step 3 — Your language

Nine options, each written in its own script. Here English is selected (orange outline + tick).

5 · Onboarding step 4 — What pulls you in?

Which apps to block. Instagram, YouTube, Facebook come pre-ticked; X, Reddit, Snapchat, TikTok available.

6 · Permission flow

One of five permission screens (Android requires each). Plain explanation + a Grant button.

7 · Home

The everyday screen: your current mantra, progress bar with Day X of 14, streak + reads-today chips, Browse library button, green 'Protection active' dot at the bottom, settings gear top-right.

8 · THE CORE FEATURE — the overlay

This is what appears the moment you open YouTube/Instagram/Facebook. Your mantra, its meaning, a 15-second countdown — only then does Continue unlock the app. This screenshot was taken with YouTube actually open underneath.

9 · Library

All 26 mantras, with four filter rows: source, length, intention, deity.

10 · Library — filtered

Tapped 'Bhagavad Gita' → only the 8 Gita verses show.

11 · Mantra detail

Tap any mantra: full text, transliteration, meaning, info chips, and the orange 'Make this my sadhana' button to switch your practice.

12 · Settings

Reachable from the gear on Home. Change your mantra, language, blocked apps, intention; switch Light/Dark/System; completion-notification toggle; version at the bottom.

13 · Settings — DARK MODE

The same screen after tapping 'Dark'. Whole app flips to the bottle-green night theme instantly.

14 · Home — dark

Home in dark mode.

15 · Library — dark

Library in dark mode — chips and cards all re-colored correctly.

16 · Home after one read

After tapping Continue on the overlay once: Day 1 of 14, streak and reads updated.

17 · The honest switch warning

If you try to switch mantras mid-journey, this dialog tells you plainly the journey starts over. (The '1 days' grammar here is already fixed in code.)

— end of walkthrough · 18 screens · build 31dbdd5 —
